septal
  • API reference
  • Convergence
  • View page source

Convergence

KKT residual computation and convergence check for the SQP solver.

Convergence is declared when both:

‖proj_{[lb,ub]}(x - ∇_xL) - x‖_∞ ≤ ε_stat (stationarity, projected gradient) max(max(g-rhs, 0), max(lhs-g, 0))_∞ ≤ ε_feas (primal feasibility)

The projected-gradient form of stationarity correctly handles active box constraints: when x[i]=lb[i] the Lagrangian gradient may be positive (pointing into the feasible region), which is NOT a KKT violation. Using the raw ‖∇_xL‖_∞ would incorrectly flag such iterates as non-stationary.

septal.jax.sqp.convergence.is_converged(stationarity, feasibility, cfg)[source]

Return True iff both KKT tolerances are satisfied.

Return type:

Array

septal.jax.sqp.convergence.kkt_residuals(x, lam, p, objective, constraints, constraint_lhs, constraint_rhs, n_g, lb=None, ub=None)[source]

Compute KKT stationarity and primal feasibility residuals.

Parameters:

  • x (Array) – Current iterate, shape (n,).

  • lam (Array) – Dual variables for general constraints, shape (n_g,).

  • p (Array) – Parameter vector, shape (m,).

  • objective (Callable) – f(x, p) -> scalar.

  • constraints (Callable | None) – g(x, p) -> (n_g,) or None.

  • constraint_lhs (Array | None) – Constraint bounds, each of shape (n_g,) or None.

  • constraint_rhs (Array | None) – Constraint bounds, each of shape (n_g,) or None.

  • n_g (int) – Number of general constraints.

  • lb (Array | None) – Decision-variable bounds, each shape (n,). When provided, the stationarity metric uses the projected-gradient residual ‖proj_{[lb,ub]}(x - ∇_xL) - x‖_∞ which correctly handles active box constraints. Pass None to fall back to ‖∇_xL‖_∞.

  • ub (Array | None) – Decision-variable bounds, each shape (n,). When provided, the stationarity metric uses the projected-gradient residual ‖proj_{[lb,ub]}(x - ∇_xL) - x‖_∞ which correctly handles active box constraints. Pass None to fall back to ‖∇_xL‖_∞.

Return type:

Tuple[Array, Array]

Returns:

  • stationarity (jnp.ndarray) – Scalar projected-gradient (or raw) stationarity residual.

  • feasibility (jnp.ndarray) – Scalar constraint violation in the L∞ norm.

KKT conditions

The solver checks two KKT residuals at each iterate.

Stationarity — projected-gradient condition accounting for active box constraints:

\[\text{stat} = \left\|\,\text{proj}_{[lb,\,ub]}\!\bigl(x - \nabla_x L(x,\lambda,p)\bigr) - x\,\right\|_\infty\]

Feasibility — L∞ constraint violation:

\[\text{feas} = \max_i\,\max\bigl(g_i(x,p) - r_i,\; \ell_i - g_i(x,p),\; 0\bigr)\]

Convergence is declared when both residuals are below their respective tolerances (tol_stationarity, tol_feasibility in SQPConfig).
